title: Plan d'un hotel particulier (Francois-Joseph Belanger)
creator: Francois-Joseph Belanger (1744-1818), draughtsman
rightsholder_or_source: Bibliotheque nationale de France (Gallica); via Wikimedia Commons
research_notes: Very high-resolution original drawing (5012x6322 native). French hotel-particulier court-and-garden layout.
research_features: Hand-drawn architect's presentation plan of a Parisian aristocratic townhouse: entrance court (cour d'honneur), main reception suite, and service rooms arranged between court and garden. Drawn in a period ink-wash convention with curved-wall salons.
feature_highlights: original hand-drawn ink-and-wash plan with pink/rose poché walls
handwritten inscribed dimensions in period French numerals
three staircases: two curved/winding plus one straight-run
curved bay / apsidal salon end in the main block
long narrow service wing of small repeated rooms
court-and-garden layout: cour d'honneur, colonnade, and garden grounds
colonnade/portico drawn as a row of dots bounding the open court
supported_examples: straight walls forming the partitioned interior rooms and service wing
handwritten linear inscribed dimensions (not a lettered legend)
raster underlay with scale calibration to trace the original drawing
roadmap_examples: straight-run stair (2D symbol) for the parallel-tread flight
wall construction profiles / poché for masonry walls
site/textual metadata for an aristocratic townhouse

gap_features.why: a salon has a curved bay/apsidal end and two stairs are curved/winding, all needing nonlinear geometry
the sheet lays out the building between an entrance court and a garden, drawing the cour d'honneur and grounds as geometry rather than only the building footprint
the colonnade/portico bounding the court is a roofed-but-open space, not a fully wall-enclosed room
two of the staircases are curved/winding rather than straight-run flights
